---
title: Prefetch
description: Warm the cache for related keys on every get.
icon: Zap
---

Whenever a key is read, the plugin fires background fetches for related keys returned by `keyFn`. Those keys are loaded into the cache before they are explicitly requested, reducing latency for predictable access patterns.

## Setup

```ts
import { prefetchPlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/prefetch";
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { redisAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/redis";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis }),
  plugins: [
    prefetchPlugin({
      keyFn: (key) => [`${key}:meta`, `${key}:tags`],
      maxKeys: 5,
    }),
  ],
});
```

## Options

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| `keyFn` | `(key: string) => string[]` | — | Returns the related keys to prefetch for a given key |
| `maxKeys` | `number` | `10` | Maximum number of related keys to prefetch per `get` |

## Notes

- Prefetched keys are fetched **in the background** — they do not block the current `get`.
- Errors from background fetches are silently ignored (`.catch(() => {})`).
- If `keyFn` is omitted, the plugin is registered but does nothing.
